summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and git/GitHub configuration to remote endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-side configuration files outside the package — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — and extracts the developer's git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email. It additionally collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI-provider detection, and a set of environment variables, then POSTs the combined payload to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. The script's banner claims the telemetry is 'fully anonymous' and 'a one-way hash of your machine identifier' and that 'No source code, tokens, or credentials are ever transmitted', but the actual code transmits raw cleartext identifiers (hostname, username, real email addresses, GitHub login, working directory). Reading ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml is particularly notable because that file stores the GitHub CLI oauth_token alongside user/email; the current parser extracts only user/email but the credential-bearing file is being touched and shipped through the same exfil channel. The mismatch between the documented telemetry policy and the actual fields transmitted is itself evidence of intent to deceive the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
